finding_results.notes: Service Corridor B shifts from abstract forecast to an on-page assault with bodily injury and stolen access materials, and that consequence directly drives immediate command notification.

Lena explicitly tests and rejects house and courthouse scenarios with evidence-based constraints, then evaluates non-blade methods (gunshot/poison) before selecting Service Corridor B as a highest-probability lane rather than certainty.

finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_12.md:26; chapters/chapter_12.md:46; chapters/chapter_12.md:48; chapters/chapter_12.md:58; chapters/chapter_12.md:88; chapters/chapter_12.md:92
finding_results.notes: Beau sets a hard non-negotiable operational boundary with immediate investigative impact before releasing key lead details, and the same condition remains active and reiterated at scene exit.

summary: Chapter 12 now carries the hospital rupture as a durable operational cost, grounds the prediction chain through explicit alternative elimination, and converts the Service Corridor B threat into embodied consequence that triggers immediate command action.
